body.title: Nanny Ogg device scaffold — BaseDevice/BaseShim + registry
body.result: Nanny Ogg registered in skeleton registry; appears in /api/device/list
body.intention: I intend that Nanny Ogg appears as a first-class device in the rack — visible in the web UI, registered in the skeleton, with a clear role boundary (cron only, no inference).
body.description:
Create devices/nanny_ogg/ with BaseDevice + BaseShim, register in the skeleton flat-file registry so she appears in /api/device/list and gets a fascia tab in the web UI. Mirror the Hubert scaffold pattern.

**Affected files:** devices/nanny_ogg/__init__.py, devices/nanny_ogg/device.py, devices/nanny_ogg/shim.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; device must inherit BaseDevice/BaseShim per structural rules
**Scope boundary:** Scaffold only — no cron wiring, no quotes table, no fascia settings logic. Out of scope: any scheduling functionality.
**Completion criteria:** `pip install -e .` succeeds; `GET /api/device/list` includes nanny_ogg; web UI shows Nanny Ogg tab with a basic fascia page.
